summaryText: Guest Pastor James Braxton Powell opened the service by contrasting the thief who comes to steal, kill, and destroy with Jesus, the Good Shepherd who gives abundant life (John 10:10). He taught that to experience victory in life's difficult 'valley' seasons, we must gain a new vision for them, understanding that God is intimately present and actively at work within our struggles. The core of the message was an exposition of Psalm 23, showing how David's declaration 'The Lord is my shepherd' provides a foundation of God's character before we enter the valley. Pastor James explained that valleys are not just low places but slow, intimate places where we stop merely talking about God and begin talking directly to Him. He emphasized that suffering, when walked through with the Shepherd, produces perseverance, character, and hope (Romans 5:3-4), much like necessary ingredients create a desired product. The sermon concluded with the powerful truth that God's goodness and mercy actively pursue us, inviting everyone—whether making a first-time decision for Jesus or a believer stuck in a valley—to come home to the Shepherd's relentless love.
mainPoints: Victory in the valley begins with knowing the character of the Good Shepherd, who provides, guides, and refreshes us (Psalm 23:1-3).
The valley is where we move from talking about God to talking to Him; it is a place of intimate growth where suffering, walked through with God, produces perseverance, character, and hope.
God's goodness and mercy actively chase us down, fighting for us and inviting our real selves into a cycle of dwelling in His presence, not just enduring a season.

versesMentioned.text: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
The LORD is my shepherd, I lack nothing.
He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake.
Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.
Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the LORD forever.
Not only so, but we also glory in our sufferings, because we know that suffering produces perseverance; perseverance, character; and character, hope.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:1 (NIV) - 'The LORD is my shepherd, I lack nothing.'
Monday.action: Write down one area where you feel lack. Next to it, write one way God has provided for you this week, even in a small way.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being my Shepherd. Help me to trust today that with you, I truly lack nothing I need.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 23:2-3 (NIV) - 'He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul.'
Tuesday.action: Intentionally pause for 5 minutes of quiet today. Don't produce, pray, or plan. Just be still and let God realign your soul.
Tuesday.prayer: Shepherd, I choose to lay down my striving. Lead me to quiet waters and refresh my soul today. Realign my heart with Yours.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 23:4 (NIV) - 'Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me.'
Wednesday.action: Identify one current 'valley' in your life. Instead of complaining about it, speak directly to God about it, acknowledging His presence with you.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I acknowledge You are walking with me in this valley. I choose not to fear, for You are my comfort and my guide.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 5:3-4 (NIV) - '...we know that suffering produces perseverance; perseverance, character; and character, hope.'
Thursday.action: Reflect on a past difficulty. Identify one quality (like patience, strength, or compassion) that grew in you as a result.
Thursday.prayer: God, help me see my current struggles through the lens of Your purpose. Produce in me perseverance, character, and unshakable hope.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 23:5 (NIV) - 'You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies.'
Friday.action: Declare God's victory over a specific fear or opposition you face. Write down, 'God is preparing a table for me here.'
Friday.prayer: Thank you, Lord, for fighting for me. You are my security. I trust you to promote and provide for me, even in challenging places.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 23:6 (NIV) - 'Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life.'
Saturday.action: Do one simple, kind act for someone else today, as an overflow of the goodness and mercy God has shown you.
Saturday.prayer: Father, I am overwhelmed that Your goodness and mercy chase me down. Let me dwell in Your house and extend Your love to others.
